reasoning: Liu Zige's 200m butterfly long course record of 2:01.81 has stood since the 2009 supersuit era. Summer McIntosh came extremely close recently, clocking 2:01.99 at the World Championships in July 2025. While she is a massive threat to the record, the likelihood of it falling between November 2025 and February 2026 is relatively low. The winter swimming calendar is largely dominated by short course events (like the World Cup series) or short course yards for US-based training. Major long course competitions usually don't take place until the spring (e.g., national trials in March/April/May). Even if someone swims at a long course meet like the December US Open or a Jan/Feb TYR Pro Swim Series, swimmers are typically not rested or peaked in the middle of their training blocks, making a world record in a gruelling event like the 200m butterfly quite rare. Therefore, the record is highly likely to survive the winter. I am decaying the probability gradually from 0.98 (1 week out) to 0.88 (3 months out) to account for the possibility of an unexpected breakthrough.
